# Arclight Painting — Complete AI Knowledge Base

> Veteran-owned professional painting company headquartered in Bothell, Washington. 4.9-star rating with 269+ verified Google reviews. Serving the greater Seattle Eastside, Snohomish County, and North King County.

---

## Company Overview

Arclight Painting is a veteran-owned, locally operated painting company headquartered at 9906 NE 204th Place, Bothell, WA 98011. The company was founded by Shawn Nagano and Jennifer Pattison, both U.S. Army veterans who built the business around discipline, trust, and doing the job right.

Shawn Nagano's painting career began in the mid-1990s, when he started painting during college summers at the University of Washington. What began as side work became a lifelong commitment to craftsmanship, integrity, and service. After building his first business and serving as a commissioned officer in the U.S. Army, Shawn founded Arclight Painting in 2013 alongside co-founder Jennifer Pattison, also a veteran. Together, they built a company rooted in honesty, ownership, friendship, and family — the four core values that guide every decision and every project.

The Arclight name reflects clarity, attention to detail, and transformation — values that continue to shape the company's culture and work today. As a veteran-owned, values-driven company based in Bothell, the team built Arclight Painting to serve their community through honesty, craftsmanship, and above-and-beyond care. Every project reflects their commitment to doing the job right and treating customers like friends, not transactions.

- **Phone:** (425) 757-1199
- **Email:** info@arclightpainting.com
- **Address:** 9906 NE 204th Place, Bothell, WA 98011
- **Hours:** Monday–Friday, 8:00 AM – 4:30 PM

### Core Values

- **Honesty** — We tell the truth, even when it's hard.
- **Ownership** — We take responsibility for our work and our word.
- **Friendship** — We treat every client and teammate like family.
- **Family** — We protect what matters most — your home and our people.

---

## Services

### Interior Painting

Your home's interior is where life happens, and every wall, ceiling, and trim piece plays a role in how it looks and feels. Arclight's interior house painting process in Bothell is built around the details that make the difference — thorough surface preparation, top-quality paints selected through independent testing, and clean, organized worksites from start to finish.

Whether you need a single accent wall or a full-home repaint, the team delivers consistent, lasting results. They protect furniture and floors, repair minor surface imperfections, and apply the right primer and paint system for every surface. The final walkthrough ensures every detail meets their standard — and yours.

Interior painting typically includes walls, ceilings, trim, doors, and closets — with the exact scope outlined in the TrueQuote™ estimate. Most interior painting projects take two to five days, depending on the number of rooms, the amount of prep work, and whether ceilings, trim, or doors are included. Paints are selected based on independent lab testing, real-world durability data, and the specific needs of each surface — not brand partnerships.

### Exterior Painting

Your home's exterior is its first line of defense against the Pacific Northwest's rain, wind, and UV exposure. Arclight's exterior house painting process in Bothell starts with the preparation that makes the difference — power washing, scraping, sanding, caulking, and priming every surface before any paint is applied.

Whether you need a full-home repaint or targeted work on trim, fascia, and siding, the team delivers a durable, beautiful finish that protects your home for years. They protect landscaping and property throughout the project and schedule around weather windows so the paint cures properly.

Exterior house painting typically includes siding, trim, fascia, soffits, doors, shutters, and other exterior surfaces. Exterior paints are selected based on independent lab testing, real-world durability data, and the specific demands of each surface — UV resistance, moisture protection, and adhesion performance determine which product goes on your home.

### Cabinet Refinishing

Kitchen cabinets endure daily wear from food, grease, and stains, which fades and scuffs them over time. Cabinet refinishing is a cost-effective way to give your kitchen a fresh, modern look without the expense of a full remodel.

The cabinet refinishing process involves careful removal of hardware, thorough cleaning, sanding, priming, and applying multiple coats of premium cabinet-grade paint or stain for a factory-smooth finish. Cabinet-grade coatings are selected based on independent testing, adhesion performance, and durability under daily kitchen use.

When properly prepared and coated with cabinet-grade products, the finish is highly durable. Significant color changes — such as dark cabinets to light colors — may require additional preparation, priming, and coats to achieve a clean, durable result. Hardware replacement or reinstallation can often be included depending on the project.

### Drywall Repair

Drywall is a key element for your home's structure and style. Whether you have nail holes, cracks, water damage, or larger patches that need repair, Arclight delivers seamless results. The team matches existing textures and finishes so repairs blend perfectly with the surrounding wall.

Repair capabilities include nail holes, dents, stress cracks, minor water damage, small holes, seam issues, and other common wall and ceiling defects. Texture matching depends on the existing finish, the size of the repair, lighting conditions, and the surrounding surface. In many cases, the best visual result comes from priming and painting the full wall or ceiling plane rather than only the patched area.

Drywall repair can be paired with priming and painting as part of the overall scope. The work can create dust, but steps are taken to contain and clean the work area as much as possible. Combined with painting services, walls look flawless from corner to corner.

### Popcorn Ceiling Removal

Popcorn ceilings are one of the most dated features in homes across the Pacific Northwest. Arclight's popcorn ceiling removal service transforms rough, textured ceilings into smooth, modern finishes that brighten rooms and increase home value.

The process involves careful containment and protection, texture removal, skim coating, sanding, priming, and painting. The experienced team handles every step so homeowners get a flawless result without the mess and hassle of doing it themselves. Popcorn texture can hide joints, patches, and surface imperfections, so after removal, ceilings need repair, skim coating, sanding, texturing, and priming before they can be painted.

Due to the number of steps in the process, popcorn ceiling removal takes a minimum of three days. If the ceiling is older, testing for asbestos may be appropriate before disturbing the material. The team can discuss this during the estimate process.

### Pressure Washing

Professional pressure washing cleans siding, driveways, decks, and walkways to restore a fresh, clean look. Years of dirt, mold, mildew, and algae buildup can make a home look aged and neglected. Pressure washing is also an essential first step before any exterior painting project, ensuring proper paint adhesion and a long-lasting finish.

Pressure washing may be appropriate for driveways, walkways, patios, some siding, fences, and other exterior surfaces. The correct method depends on the material and condition. Pressure washing uses stronger water pressure, while soft washing uses lower pressure with cleaning solutions for more delicate surfaces. The team chooses the method based on what is safest and most effective.

Using the wrong pressure or technique can damage paint, wood, siding, or force water where it should not go. Proper methods matter. Most homes in the Pacific Northwest benefit from annual or biannual cleaning to prevent mold, mildew, and algae from taking hold.

### Commercial Painting

Commercial spaces need more than a fresh coat of paint — they need a professional result delivered on a timeline that respects business operations. Arclight's commercial painting services in Bothell are designed around the client's schedule, standards, and space.

From offices and retail spaces to occupied commercial properties, Arclight delivers clean, professional results with minimal disruption. After-hours and weekend scheduling, phased project plans, and clear communication keep businesses running while the team works. The company works on a variety of commercial spaces including offices, retail spaces, tenant improvements, common areas, light industrial properties, and other business environments.

Commercial work can often be scheduled in phases, after hours, or on weekends to reduce disruption. Arclight carries the proper insurance and operates professionally to support commercial clients and property managers. Maintenance painting can help commercial properties stay clean, consistent, and protected over time.

### Painter for a Day (One-Day Painting Service)

The Painter-for-a-Day service gives homeowners a skilled, background-checked painter dedicated to their home for a full 8 hours. Perfect for touch-ups, small projects, or that one room you've been meaning to update. No estimate needed — just book and go.

You provide the checklist, Arclight provides the painter. From touch-up work and accent walls to painting entry doors and repairing trim, the Painter-for-a-Day handles it all. Smaller, well-defined scopes are the best fit, such as a single room, accent walls, touch-ups, or selected trim and door work. Quality depends on proper planning, realistic scope, surface preparation, and having the right crew for the job.

Pricing is straightforward: one painter is $698, and two painters are $1,396. The crew will outline any needed paint or area prep before the appointment so they can begin efficiently and stay productive throughout the day.

### Color Consultation (ColorConfidence™)

ColorConfidence™ Consultation is a guided process that includes expert color selection, visual mockups of your real home, custom color matching, and on-site test patches when needed. Homeowners also receive a full ColorConfidence™ Blueprint with final color specs and placement plan, so they can choose confidently and paint beautifully.

A 30-minute virtual consultation is free and included with any painting project. A 60-minute in-home consultation is $195. Each option is tailored to the homeowner's needs. The service helps narrow options, reduce overwhelm, and guide toward the choice that fits the home, lighting, style, and goals best.

The expert colorist evaluates the current paint or finish and guides homeowners toward the best match, considering paint type, brand, sheen, tinting, and fading. On-site test patches are valuable because they let homeowners see how the color actually reads in their home before making a final decision.

---

## The Above & Beyond Blueprint

The Above & Beyond Blueprint is Arclight's proven, structured process that ensures consistent, high-quality results on every project. It is the system behind the smooth experience, clear communication, and exceptional craftsmanship that clients consistently praise.

### Step 1: TrueQuote™ Pricing

Every project begins with a detailed, on-site evaluation. The team walks through every room or surface, measures walls, ceilings, trim, siding, and all paintable areas, and builds a detailed, fixed-price proposal. Every line item is explained so the homeowner understands exactly what they are paying for and why. Once the scope is approved, the estimate becomes a fixed-price agreement with no hidden fees and no surprises.

### Step 2: PowerFlex™ Scheduling

PowerFlex™ Scheduling uses accurate labor forecasting, smart crew sizing, and professional coordination built around the homeowner's calendar. For interior projects, rooms are scheduled individually so the homeowner can keep living in their home. For exterior projects, work is planned around optimal weather windows and adjusted for rain delays so the paint cures properly. Clear timelines and daily updates keep homeowners informed at every step.

### Step 3: ColorConfidence™ Consultation

Homeowners choose colors through a guided process with the in-house colorist. The process includes visual mockups of the homeowner's actual rooms or exterior, expert guidance on undertones and lighting, and on-site test patches when needed. The homeowner receives a full ColorConfidence™ Blueprint with final color specs and placement plan.

### Step 4: JobTrack™ Project Management

Real-time tracking from setup through final coat. Homeowners always know which areas are prepped, primed, and painted. Daily updates and proactive communication ensure the homeowner is never left wondering about progress or next steps.

### Step 5: Surface Preparation & Protection

Every project starts with thorough surface protection and preparation. This includes protecting floors, furniture, fixtures, and landscaping. Surfaces are filled, sanded, scraped, caulked, primed, and repaired as needed. For exterior projects, power washing removes dirt, chalking, mildew, and loose material before paint is applied. For interior projects, holes are filled, rough spots sanded, minor drywall damage repaired, gaps caulked, trim masked, and floors and furniture protected.

### Step 6: Top-Quality Paint Application

Walls are rolled with even, consistent coats. Trim and edges are cut in by hand. Top-quality paints — selected through independent testing rather than brand partnerships — deliver richer color, better coverage, and a finish that holds up to daily life. For exterior projects, siding, trim, and fascia are coated using the right method for each surface — brush, roll, or spray.

### Step 7: PCA™ Quality Inspection

Before any project is considered complete, it receives an independent review by the Quality Supervisor based on Painting Contractors of America Industry Standards. The supervisor walks every room or surface with the homeowner, inspecting walls, trim, ceilings, and all painted surfaces. If anything falls short, it is documented and corrected before the job is closed.

---

## Service Areas

Arclight Painting serves the following cities and communities in Washington State:

### Bothell (Headquarters)

Bothell is home base for Arclight Painting. The team lives, works, and raises their families here. Bothell homes range from 1970s cedar-sided split-levels in established neighborhoods to modern fiber-cement builds in Canyon Park. Each siding type demands different prep. Many Bothell neighborhoods like Canyon Park, Queensgate, and North Creek have HOA architectural guidelines governing exterior colors. Key neighborhoods include Canyon Park, North Creek, Queensgate, Bothell Landing, Brickyard, and Maltby.

### Kirkland

Kirkland's housing stock is one of the most architecturally diverse on the Eastside — from early-1900s Craftsman bungalows in Norkirk and Houghton to modern luxury builds in Bridle Trails. Homes near Lake Washington in Juanita and Moss Bay face elevated humidity and salt-air exposure. Kirkland hillside homes present unique access challenges with steep lots and multi-story rear elevations. Key neighborhoods include Juanita, Houghton, Totem Lake, Bridle Trails, Norkirk, and Moss Bay.

### Bellevue

Bellevue has the highest concentration of custom-built luxury homes on the Eastside. Properties in West Bellevue, Meydenbauer, and Bridle Trails feature complex trim profiles, 20-foot ceilings, specialty finishes, and premium materials. The Bel-Red corridor and downtown Bellevue are home to a growing commercial market. Key neighborhoods include Meydenbauer, West Bellevue, Bridle Trails, Wilburton, Bel-Red, and Crossroads.

### Redmond

Redmond's established neighborhoods like Education Hill and Grass Lawn feature homes from the 1970s through 1990s with a mix of cedar siding, T1-11 panels, and early composite materials. The tech-driven workforce means many homeowners have demanding schedules. Key neighborhoods include Education Hill, Grass Lawn, Overlake, Southeast Redmond, Idylwood, and Willows.

### Woodinville

Woodinville's estate-style properties on Hollywood Hill feature large exterior surfaces — often 4,000 to 6,000+ square feet of paintable area. The wine country aesthetic means homeowners often choose rich, earthy color palettes. Many properties feature extensive natural wood — cedar siding, exposed beams, wood shingles, and stained decks. Key neighborhoods include Hollywood Hill, Woodinville Proper, Wellington, Cottage Lake area, Warehouse District, and Bear Creek.

### Kenmore

Kenmore sits at the north tip of Lake Washington, creating a unique microclimate for exterior painting. Homes in Northshore and along the Burke-Gilman Trail face constant moisture from the lake. Many Kenmore homes are mid-century ramblers and split-levels built in the 1960s and 1970s with original cedar siding. Key neighborhoods include Kenmore Village, Northshore, Arrowhead, Linwood, St. Edward, and the Burke-Gilman corridor.

### Mill Creek

Mill Creek is a master-planned community with some of the most detailed HOA architectural guidelines in the region. The homes — particularly in Country Club, Heatherwood, and The Farm — are well-maintained two-story structures with complex rooflines, multiple gables, and detailed trim. Mill Creek's 15+ miles of trails wind directly behind many homes, creating shaded rear elevations. Key neighborhoods include Country Club, Heatherwood, The Farm, Thomas Lake, Penny Creek, and Gateway.

### Everett

Everett's Rucker Hill neighborhood is home to some of the finest Victorian and Craftsman homes in Snohomish County, many dating to the early 1900s. The Port of Everett marina area brings salt air, wind-driven rain, and coastal humidity. Everett's growing commercial corridor needs painting contractors who can work around business operations. Key neighborhoods include Rucker Hill, Bayside, Boulevard Bluffs, Glacier View, Lowell, and Millwright District.

### Lake Forest Park

Lake Forest Park's defining characteristic is its towering evergreen canopy. Homes sit under 80- to 100-foot Douglas firs and western red cedars that keep surfaces shaded and damp year-round. The mid-century homes — built primarily in the 1950s through 1970s — have unique architectural details including original wood siding, large picture windows, and low-slope rooflines. Key neighborhoods include Town Center, Sheridan Beach, Horizon View, Brookside, and the Burke-Gilman Trail area.

### Cottage Lake

Cottage Lake properties sit under heavy evergreen canopy that keeps exterior surfaces shaded and damp for much of the year. Homes tend to be larger than typical suburban properties, often on half-acre to multi-acre lots with outbuildings, detached garages, and extensive deck and fence surfaces. Many homes feature natural cedar siding and wood shingles. Key neighborhoods include Cottage Lake, Avondale, Bear Creek area, Redmond Ridge border, and Woodinville outskirts.

### Union Hill-Novelty Hill

Union Hill and Novelty Hill homes are among the largest in the service area — many range from 3,000 to 5,000+ square feet with open floor plans, vaulted ceilings, and two-story great rooms. Most homes were built in the 2000s and 2010s with builder-grade paint that shows wear after 10-15 years. Key neighborhoods include Redmond Ridge, Trilogy at Redmond Ridge, Novelty Hill, Union Hill, and Bear Creek area.

### Clearview

Clearview's unincorporated setting means larger lots, longer driveways, and properties that sit further from the road — often surrounded by mature trees. The Highway 9 corridor includes homes with outbuildings, barns, fences, and detached workshops. Clearview's semi-rural environment means more exposure to wind, debris, and organic growth. Key neighborhoods include Clearview, Maltby border, Highway 9 corridor, Cathcart, and unincorporated Snohomish County.

### Seattle

Seattle's architectural diversity is unmatched — Craftsman bungalows in Wallingford, Queen Anne Victorians on Capitol Hill, Tudor-style homes in Montlake, and modern infill construction in Ballard and Fremont. Seattle's maritime climate delivers 150+ days of measurable rain per year. Older homes — particularly pre-1978 construction — may contain lead paint, and Arclight follows EPA RRP lead-safe work practices on all qualifying homes. Key neighborhoods include Ballard, Wallingford, Fremont, Capitol Hill, Green Lake, Ravenna, University District, and West Seattle.

---

## Trust Signals & Credentials

- **4.9-star rating** with 269+ verified Google reviews
- **Veteran-owned** and locally operated
- **BBB A+ rated** member
- **PCA Legacy Member** — Painting Contractors of America
- **Licensed, bonded, and insured** in Washington State
- **OMWBE certified** — Office of Minority and Women's Business Enterprises
- **100% Satisfaction Guarantee** on every project
- **5-Year FreshStart™ Touch-Up Plan** — free workmanship coverage including touch-ups for peeling, blistering, or chipping paint
- **PCA™ Quality Inspection** — every project independently inspected by the Quality Supervisor against Painting Contractors of America Industry Standards

---

## Frequently Asked Questions

### General

**What painting services does Arclight Painting offer?**
Arclight Painting offers interior painting, exterior painting, cabinet refinishing, drywall repair, pressure washing, commercial painting, one-day painting service, and popcorn ceiling removal. We handle everything from simple repaints to more involved projects that require repairs and detailed preparation.

**Are you licensed, bonded, and insured?**
Yes. Arclight Painting is licensed, bonded, and insured so you can move forward with confidence knowing your project is in professional hands.

**Do you offer free estimates or fixed-price quotes?**
Yes. We provide free estimates and detailed written proposals. Once your scope is approved, the estimate becomes a fixed-price agreement.

**Why do painting quotes vary so much?**
Quotes vary because not every painter includes the same preparation, materials, workmanship, warranty, or insurance coverage. Lower bids often leave out important steps that affect how the final result looks and how long it lasts.

**What is included in your painting process?**
Our process includes a detailed estimate, surface protection, project-specific prep, the proper primer and paint system, daily cleanup, and a final walkthrough. The exact scope depends on your project, but the goal is always a smooth process and lasting results.

**Do you stand behind your work?**
Yes. We back our work with a 100% Satisfaction Guarantee, and we verify the final result through a walkthrough based on PCA™ Quality Inspection. Every project is independently inspected by our Quality Supervisor according to Painting Contractors of America Industry Standards, and if anything falls short, we document it and correct it before the job is considered complete. For added peace of mind, we also include our 5-Year FreshStart™ Touch-Up Plan.

**How soon can you start?**
Most projects can be scheduled within one to three weeks, depending on the season, project size, and current workload.

**How long will my project take?**
Most interior painting projects take two to five days, and exterior projects may take three to seven days or more depending on size, prep, and weather. With PowerFlex™ Scheduling, we turn that into a clear, realistic plan using accurate labor forecasting, smart crew sizing, and professional coordination built around your calendar.

**How do I choose the right paint colors?**
Start with a ColorConfidence™ Consultation — a guided process that includes expert color selection, visual mockups of your real home, custom color matching, and on-site test patches when needed.

**How much will painting my house cost?**
The cost depends on the size of your home, the condition of the surfaces, and the scope of work involved. Every TrueQuote™ is built around your specific project with clear, itemized pricing and no hidden fees.

### Interior Painting

**What does interior house painting include in Bothell?**
Interior house painting typically includes walls, ceilings, trim, doors, and closets — with the exact scope outlined in your TrueQuote™ estimate. We handle all surface preparation, priming, painting, and cleanup so you get a complete, move-in-ready result.

**Do you protect floors and furniture during interior painting?**
Yes. We cover all floors with drop cloths and protect furniture, fixtures, and trim with plastic and masking before any painting begins.

**Can you repair drywall before painting?**
Yes. Minor drywall repair — including nail holes, dents, and small cracks — is included in the prep process. For larger drywall damage, we can scope the repair into your TrueQuote™ estimate.

**After painting a room, how long should I wait before sleeping in it?**
With standard latex paint and good ventilation, most rooms are safe to sleep in after 24 hours. Low-VOC and zero-VOC paints can cut that wait to just a few hours.

### Exterior Painting

**Do you pressure wash before exterior painting?**
In most cases, yes. Exterior surfaces need washing to remove dirt, chalking, mildew, and loose material before paint can properly adhere.

**What time of year is best for exterior painting in Bothell?**
Exterior painting is best completed during favorable weather conditions. With PowerFlex™ Scheduling, we plan around optimal weather windows and adjust for rain delays so the paint cures properly.

**What happens if it rains during my exterior project?**
We plan carefully around the weather. If conditions are not right for proper application or drying, we adjust the schedule rather than compromise the result.

### Cabinet Refinishing

**What is the difference between refinishing, repainting, and refacing?**
Refinishing typically refers to renewing the existing cabinet surface. Repainting focuses on changing the color or finish. Refacing involves replacing doors, drawer fronts, or exterior skins while keeping the cabinet boxes.

**How durable is cabinet refinishing?**
When cabinets are properly cleaned, prepared, primed, and coated with cabinet-grade products, the finish can be very durable. Proper curing and normal care are important to long-term performance.

### Popcorn Ceiling Removal

**What is popcorn ceiling removal?**
Popcorn ceiling removal is the process of removing textured acoustic ceiling material to create a flatter, more updated appearance.

**Will the ceiling need repairs after removal?**
Yes. Popcorn texture can hide joints, patches, and surface imperfections. After removal, ceilings need repair, skim coating, sanding, texturing, and priming before they can be painted.

**Should I test older popcorn ceilings for asbestos before removal?**
If the ceiling is older, testing for asbestos may be appropriate before disturbing the material.
